title: Super inflows drive Australian Ethical to FUM record
full_text:
Australian Ethical has recorded a new funds under management (FUM) milestone of $14.5 billion after strong end-of-financial-year superannuation inflows helped offset challenging market conditions and weakness across parts of its investment business.
The ethical investment manager reported total FUM rose from $13.57 billion at the end of March to $14.5 billion at 30 June, supported by organic net inflows of $170 million during the fourth quarter and favourable market movements that added $650 million.
Superannuation remained the key growth driver, with net inflows of $196 million during the quarter lifting super FUM to $10.4 billion. Retail and wholesale investment products, however, recorded net outflows of $26 million.
Managing director John McMurdo said strong superannuation flows and product development had enabled the business to finish the financial year on a high despite difficult conditions.
“Fourth quarter flows into superannuation and new product innovation in our asset management business have allowed us to finish strongly in FY26. The new FUM milestone is a pleasing result given the substantial economic and market challenges of FY26.
“The decision to build out our investment team and diversify asset classes over recent years, was rewarded in what has been a challenging year for many fund managers, with Australian Ethical achieving positive returns across most of our portfolios, and net-inflows into core product categories.
“During the period, we have continued to see strong demand for our approach to investing, with new super member joins increasing in the second half of the year. We have also seen demand growing for our newly launched Growth Opportunities Fund.
“The diversification and resilience of our business model continues to allow us to grow, even in periods of market stress.”
The fund said stronger superannuation contributions, rollover activity and rising compulsory superannuation guarantee payments underpinned quarterly inflows.
It also attributed improving member acquisition to the completion of its GROW platform migration, the reactivation of its employment platform acquisition channel and stronger digital marketing performance, which lifted new member joins during the second half of the financial year.
Outside superannuation, the March launch of the Australian Ethical Growth Opportunities Fund received a $125 million investment from the Clean Energy Finance Corporation, helping support institutional inflows and providing resilience against higher-than-usual redemptions from advised and direct investment channels during the period of market volatility.
Across the full financial year, Australian Ethical generated positive retail and wholesale net flows of $491 million alongside institutional net inflows of $173 million.
Those gains were partly offset by the previously disclosed institutional outflow linked to the Australian Unity Bank mandate, leaving total FUM at $14.5 billion compared with $13.94 billion a year earlier.
The company said FY26 investment performance reflected heightened geopolitical uncertainty and difficult market conditions. Rising oil and gas prices driven by the conflict in the Middle East added to inflationary pressures and contributed to higher interest rates, affecting asset classes unevenly.
Australian Ethical said its investment approach meant it had limited exposure to sectors that performed strongly during the year, while areas where it maintained overweight positions, including information technology, healthcare and smaller emerging companies, experienced a sell-off.
Strong returns from fixed income and private markets portfolios partially offset those headwinds, with investment performance contributing $650 million during the final quarter.
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation and Investments
page_title: Super inflows drive Australian Ethical to FUM record - Super Review
